children.label: A
children.text: operate contract schools under title XI of the Education Amendments of 1978 [ et seq.] and notify the Secretary of their election to operate the schools with assistance under this chapter rather than continuing the schools as contract schools;25 U.S.C. 2000

children.label: B
children.text: operate other tribally controlled schools eligible for assistance under this chapter and submit applications (which are approved by their tribal governing bodies) to the Secretary for such grants; or

children.label: C
children.text: elect to assume operation of Bureau-funded schools with the assistance under this chapter and submit applications (which are approved by their tribal governing bodies) to the Secretary for such grants.

children.heading: Deposit of funds
children.label: 2 Deposit of funds
children.text: Grants provided under this chapter shall be deposited into the general operating fund of the tribally controlled school with respect to which the grant is made.

children.label: i
children.text: school operations, academic, educational, residential, guidance and counseling, and administrative purposes; and

children.label: ii
children.text: support services for the school, including transportation.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: Grants provided under this chapter may, at the discretion of the school board of the tribally controlled school with respect to which such grant is provided, be used to defray operations and maintenance expenditures for the school if any funds for the operation and maintenance of the school are allocated to the school under the provisions of any of the laws described in .section 2504(a) of this title

children.heading: One grant per tribe or organization per fiscal year
children.label: 1 One grant per tribe or organization per fiscal year
children.text: Not more than one grant may be provided under this chapter with respect to any Indian tribe or tribal organization for any fiscal year.

children.heading: Nonsectarian use
children.label: 2 Nonsectarian use
children.text: Funds provided under any grant made under this chapter may not be used in connection with religious worship or sectarian instruction.

children.heading: Administrative costs limitation
children.label: 3 Administrative costs limitation
children.text: Funds provided under any grant under this chapter may not be expended for administrative costs (as defined in section 1128(h)(1) of the Education Amendments of 1978 []) in excess of the amount generated for such costs under section 1128 of such Act.25 U.S.C. 2008(h)(1)

children.heading: Limitation on transfer of funds among school sites
children.label: c Limitation on transfer of funds among school sites
A
children.text: 10 percent of the funds allocated for another school site under section 1128 of the Education Amendments of 1978 []; or25 U.S.C. 2008

children.label: B
children.text: $400,000 of the funds allocated for another school site.

children.heading: Definition of school site
children.label: 2 Definition of school site
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “school site” means the physical location and the facilities of an elementary or secondary educational or residential program operated by, or under contract or grant with, the Bureau for which a discreet student count is identified under the funding formula established under section 1127 of the Education Amendments of 1978 [].125 U.S.C. 2007So in original. Probably should be “discrete”.1

children.heading: No requirement to accept grants
children.label: d No requirement to accept grants

children.text: to require a tribe or tribal organization to apply for or accept; or
to allow any person to coerce any tribe or tribal organization to apply for, or accept,

children.heading: No effect on Federal responsibility
children.label: e No effect on Federal responsibility
children.text: Grants provided under this chapter shall not terminate, modify, suspend, or reduce the responsibility of the Federal Government to provide a program.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Whenever a tribal governing body requests retrocession of any program for which assistance is provided under this chapter, such retrocession shall become effective upon a date specified by the Secretary that is not later than 120 days after the date on which the tribal governing body requests the retrocession. A later date may be specified if mutually agreed upon by the Secretary and the tribal governing body. If such a program is retroceded, the Secretary shall provide to any Indian tribe served by such program at least the same quantity and quality of services that would have been provided under such program at the level of funding provided under this chapter prior to the retrocession.

children.heading: Status after retrocession
children.label: 2 Status after retrocession
children.text: The tribe requesting retrocession shall specify whether the retrocession is to status as a Bureau-operated school or as a school operated under contract under the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act [ et seq.].25 U.S.C. 5301

children.label: A
children.text: with assistance under this chapter; or

children.label: B
children.text: upon assumption of operation of the program under this chapter, if the school was a Bureau-funded school under title XI of the Education Amendments of 1978 [ et seq.] before receiving assistance under this chapter.25 U.S.C. 2000

children.heading: Prohibition of termination for administrative convenience
children.label: g Prohibition of termination for administrative convenience
children.text: Grants provided under this chapter may not be terminated, modified, suspended, or reduced solely for the convenience of the administering agency.
